scores.correctness_notes: Gets single-base platforms syntax right, but misses several supported bases (ubuntu@24.10, ubuntu@25.04, almalinux@9) and the modern multi-base approach entirely. Incorrectly treats the legacy `bases` key as the only multi-base option, when the gold standard shows modern `platforms` syntax supports embedded bases like `ubuntu@22.04:amd64:`.
scores.hallucination_notes: `charmcraft pack --base-index N` flag not mentioned in gold standard (potentially hallucinated); incorrectly labels `bases` key as 'deprecated' when gold standard doesn't say that; the modern multi-base platform syntax (e.g., `ubuntu@22.04:amd64:` as a platform key) is absent and replaced with an older approach presented as current guidance.
